VARIOUS ANGLES as the hands shape the clay into the form of a woman. With the help of a butter knife and some toothpicks, a little "doll" emerges -- five or six inches long, primitive, a littly chubby, but clearly a woman.
INT. SUKIE RIDGEMONT'S HOUSE - BEDROOM - DAY

Sukie's five children are getting ready for school. Havoc reigns as they fall over each other grabbing for clothes and slamming in and out of the bathroom. FOLLOW BOBBY, the five year old, as he runs out of the bedroom.
ANGLE - STAIRWAY
as Bobby comes down the stairs and goes into...
INT. KITCHEN - BOBBY - DAY
opens the door to the broom closet to check on... A cat and her nine newborn kittens. CAMERA LEAVES Bobby and MOVES OUT of the kitchen to discover.
EXT. SUKIE'S HOUSE - DAY

SUKIE RIDGEMONT is picking zucchini in her garden. In fact, the entire garden is overrun with this single vegetable. It looks like a jungle. And the zucchini are huge. She carries them like logs. Finally she stops and looks at the garden.

ANGLE - BOBBY AND MARY ANN AND OTHER KIDS Coming out of the house carrying schoolbooks and lunch pails. As Sukie hustles them into the car, she dumps the zucchini into the backseat. Bobby examines one of the sandwiches from his pail. What's this?
Peanut butter.
Peanut butter and what?
Peanut butter and jelly.
What kind of jelly? Zucchini jelly. It's very good. You'll like it.
We had zucchini yesterday, too. We had zucchini all summer. I know, honey. They just keep growing. Maybe we'll get a good frost one of these nights and they'll all die.
They drive away.
INT. WORKROOM - WOMAN'S HANDS - DAY
use a toothpick to poke a "navel" into the clay figure and then just a hint of a cleft between the legs.
EXT. PLAYGROUND - JANE SMART - DAY
is assembling the grammar school band for the opening day assembly.
We can have one quick run through if we hurry. Don't drag the tuba, Martha.
of the school, RAYMOND NEFF, approaches her from behind. He has a bit of a lecherous look in his eyes. He stands uncomfortably close to Jane.
How are we doing?
Fine, Raymond. We're doing just fine.
I wish we could afford to keep you here full time.
I wish you could, too, Raymond. I could use the money. Especially now.
Is the divorce final yet?
Yes.
Finished. Yeah. Today. I'm a free woman. I could really use the job.
Well, let's put our heads together. Maybe we can work something out. Hmm?
Very discreetly, he pats her on the behind and moves off. Jane gives him a look.
INT. ALEX'S KITCHEN - DAY

ALEXANDRA SPOFFORD is carrying two trays filled with the little clay dolls to her kiln which is next to her stove. She puts the trays into the kiln and checks the temperature. (Like the houses of Jane and Sukie, Alex's house was once nice but now suffers from Alex's economic condition which is close to poverty level.) Alex's twelveyear-old daughter, CAROL, is at the table eating her breakfast and reading a book. She is shy, introverted and slightly overweight.
Are you coming today?
Alex hurries to iron a blouse and a skirt, make coffee and toast and pack Carol's lunch -- all at the same time.
I'll be there. I just need to clean up and change. Answer the phone, will you, honey?
The phone is not ringing. Nonetheless, Carol goes to the phone -- still reading her book -- and puts her hand over the receiver. The PHONE RINGS. She picks it up. Hello?
Tell her I'm busy and can I call her back.
(into phone) She said she's busy and can she call you back. Okay.
Carol hangs up the phone and returns to the table.
Here's your lunch.
No response from Carol. with her. Alex sits down at the table
(continuing) What's the matter? Hmn?
Are you going to get married again?
Well, gee, I don't know. thought about it. Why? Nothing.
I haven't
Were you thinking about Daddy? No answer from Carol. Yeah.
(continuing) I miss him, too.
The school bus HONKS its HORN.
(continuing) You better get a move on.
Carol grabs her things and starts to go.
(continuing) You're the prettiest kid I ever had.
She kisses her. Carol goes. Alex stops her.
EXT. ELEMENTARY SCHOOL - ANGLE - FELICIA GABRIAL - DAY

is singing "America, the Beautiful." ANGLE - JANE leading the band as they try bravely to play the music. They hit some real clinkers, but Felicia sings on undaunted -- and quite beautifully. ANGLE - SCENE Students, faculty and parents -- mostly mothers -- listening. Carol, Bobby, Mary Ann and the rest of Sukie's children are among the students. The band comes to a startling finish. Everyone applauds. Felicia bows. Then she shakes hands with Jane. Jane mimes an apology and returns to her seat. She is exhausted by the experience. Felicia is unphased by the playing of the band. She sits down happily.
comes forward and addresses the assembly.
Well, wonderful. Thank you, Felicia. Thank you, children. And thank you, Miss Smart. I can see we all have our work cut out for us...
Jane winces. Some laughter comes from the audience. Raymond winks at Jane. Jane looks at him with disdain.
(continuing) But seriously, welcome. As we begin another year at Lenox Elementary, my thoughts turn -as indeed they must -- to the great family who founded the
town of Eastwick -- the Lenox
Listening to the principal with growing annoyance and

boredom. She looks out over the audience. HER POV - SUKIE Who is looking at Jane and smiling. She rolls her eyes in agreement with Jane's thoughts. Then she looks over her shoulder at... SUKIE'S POV - ALEX exchanges a look with Sukie. The three women seem to be reading each other's thoughts. Alex turns to look at... ALEX'S POV - JANE Who is smiling at Alex. Who has been speaking over the above...

In these days of disintegrating values and lost mores, when our children are exposed daily to the hazards of divorce and separation and families torn asunder, it is comforting to think back to the days...
Who is smiling but having trouble keeping her eyes open.
Who gazes toward the heavens. She closes her eyes and seems to be saying something to herself. There aren't many clouds in the sky. But the few that are there begin to move and grow in size.
... When a family like the Lenox family strove together to subdue the Indians and the thousand catastrophies of the wilderness to establish our little village...
Restless and yawning as Raymond drones on. Checking her watch and then looking up at the sky. Yawning and then looking up at the sky. Growing dark. A wind comes up out of nowhere.
... A family without whose guidance and moral purpose Eastwick would never have survived the darker periods of New England history... THUNDER rolls in the sky.
Who puts out her palm. A drop of rain falls on it. She closes her hand over the drop and looks at... HER POV - RAYMOND
The values of that time embodied in a man like Major Lenox...
A sudden CRACK of LIGHTNING and the rain starts to fall

Everyone starts to scatter. ANGLE - BOLT OF LIGHTNING strikes a tree. A large section breaks off and falls on... Standing at his podium, looking confused, surrounded by the fallen branches of the tree. He looks up at the sky as the rain continues to fall on him. ANGLE - EVERYONE ELSE running for shelter. All looking at Raymond, surprised at what's just happened.
EXT. SUKIE'S HOUSE - NIGHT
Rain is falling hard now. THUNDER and lightning play havoc in the black sky. LEGEND ON THE SCREEN: "THE COVEN."

Running from their car to the house, screaming with laughter as the rain hits them. As Sukie opens it for them and they rush into:

God.
Do you believe this?

That's right, honey. they call it. That's what
They shake themselves off, checking their hair in a mirror.

Really, the same boring speech... I was praying for it. I thought, God, he's going to go on forever... I know. thing.
I was thinking the same
I was thinking he was all wet and wouldn't it be nice if we could get out of there.
Were you really? And then it rained. I know.
That's amazing.
Well, it couldn't have happened to a nicer guy. How about a drink?
INT. SUKIE'S KITCHEN - CLOSE ON LARGE MARTINI PITCHER

Vodka is being poured into the pitcher. And then a splash of vermouth is added. (There is a hint of ritual in the mixing of these drinks -- as there often is when a martini is made.) He made a pass at me last week, too. Raymond?
Oh, God.

easily.

wiping three glasses with a small towel. Really. It was broad daylight and his wife was like ten feet away. Poor woman.

CLOSE ON ALEX'S HANDS stirring the contents of the pitcher with a glass rod. Lightning brightens the room for a moment and then THUNDER rolls.
What do you think? they still... Yes. Really?
Do you think
I don't believe it. It's true. Once a week. Honest. He told me. He said she has to have it once a week or she starts throwing things.
How could she stand it? saurkraut in heat.
He's like The ritual continues during the above as Jane drops the three olives into the three glasses and Alex pours the martinis.
Well, at least she's getting something once a week.
Heartburn. Yeah.
I mean it. Even Raymond Neff is beginning to look good to me. I know what you mean.
They toast.
Cheers.
(to Jane) And congratulations. That's right. Yeah. I forgot.
Free at last, free at last. Join the club.
They laugh and drink. A bolt of lightning seems to pass through the room. They jump and laugh even harder. Then Jane starts to cry. The other two embrace her.
I'm going to start all over again. I've been doing this all day. Don't.
Come on.
I know.
You're going to be okay. You know? It's a beginning. Not an ending.
That's what they all say. Yeah.
INT. SUKIE'S LIVING ROOM - THREE WOMEN - NIGHT (LATER)
The women are very relaxed and a little drunk.
I don't know. I still feel like I'm being unfaithful. Even if I just think about it.
It's been over a year since Monty left. I know.
And he deserted you. you with five kids.
He left
It's still hard, though. Even with the divorce, now. I keep telling myself, okay, it's time to start over again. But it's not so easy.

Well, we're a little young yet to just throw away the key. It's not natural. And I don't mean just sex. I mean, what are we doing with ourselves? I loved Ozzie, but I don't think he'd want me to be in mourning for the rest of my life, having drinks with the girls every Thursday night. What kind of life is that?
But I like our Thursday nights. It's one of the few chances we get to just, you know, relax.
Yeah, but sometimes I think we're hiding. What from?
Raymond Neff.
Okay, but it doesn't have to be Raymond Neff. Well, who?
SUKIE Somebody nice. Somebody you could like. God, it's like being a virgin all over again.
Somebody you could talk to. Somebody with a brain.
Somebody you could be yourself with. That would be a relief.
I think it would have to be somebody from out of town.
Definitely. Especially considering what's in town.
A stranger. That would be interesting.
Yeah. Romantic. Foreign. A tall dark European, traveling under a curse. In Eastwick?
Why not? A foreign prince, riding a great black horse.
Alex stirs her drink with her olive.
Why not? Let's have it
CLOSE ON SWIRLING LIQUID in the glass.
EXT. COUNTRY ROAD - NIGHT
A black Mercedes limousine is moving fast through the rain. As it rips OUT of FRAME, a flash of lightning illuminates a road sign which reads "Eastwick."
INT. SUKIE'S LIVING ROOM - THREE WOMEN - NIGHT
are as they were. He should be handsome.
Not too handsome. Nice eyes. Nice ass.
They laugh.
EXT. LENOX HOUSE - NIGHT
Rising darkly and majestically in the storm -- a great deserted mansion sitting by the sea.
ANGLE - BLACK LIMO
Approaching the house.
INT. SUKIE'S LIVING ROOM
No. Small. Really, I prefer small. Aesthetically. And huge can be a problem. Sam was huge and there were days I just couldn't face it.
I'm sort of in the middle. But honestly, what's the difference, as long as it works...
EXT. LENOX HOUSE - NIGHT

The Mercedes is still now. A chauffeur gets out and carrying an umbrella, moves around the car to open the back door. A man gets out. But both the man and the chauffeur are concealed by the umbrella as they move toward the house.
INT. SUKIE'S LIVING ROOM
I guess not.
Anyway... I don't think men are really the answer to everything.
They may not be the answer... but they are the question.
They lift their glasses to toast. As they move toward each other. Just as they "clink," lightning flashes, THUNDER rolls and we...
EXT. LENOX HOUSE - NIGHT
The house is dark for a split second and then all the lights in every room are suddenly switched on. The house has come alive. A loud piercing SCREAM is heard as we...
INT. FELICIA AND CLYDE GABRIELS' BEDROOM - ANGLE FELICIA - NIGHT
Sitting upright in bed screaming. CLYDE fumbles -surprised and half asleep -- and switches on a light. FELICIA's scream comes to an abrupt end. Felicia!?!
(very calm and totally unaware of the fact that she just screamed) What is it, Clyde?
Are you all right? What's the matter? What? Are you okay? Yes.
I'm fine.
You were yelling.
(yawning) You must have been dreaming. Go to sleep, for God's sake. It's late.
She rolls over and goes to sleep.
Clyde is wide awake
EXT. TOWN SQUARE - JANE - DAY

is walking across the square carrying her cello case. Jane carries her cello into the church. in front of the church announcing Sunday's sermon: Eastwick Quartet WEDNESDAY, 7:30 And below that, Sunday's sermon: "Anthropomorphic Evil: Is the devil a real person?"
INT. NEWSPAPER OFFICE - SUKIE - DAY
is at her desk talking to Alex.
INT. ALEX'S WORKROOM - ALEX - DAY
is handpainting a series of little pottery dolls. Sukie's news has triggered something in her.
I guess I heard you the first time. Who is he? From New York. No wife or family. He told the people at Pearly Realty he needed all that room for his pianos. Apparently, he has a lot of pianos.
How many pianos can a man have? I don't know. Do you think it's funny? I mean, strange? I mean ... you know.
What's his name? That's funny, too. They told me his name, for the column, you know. And I forgot it. (MORE)

So I called them back and they told me again and it's gone right out of my head again. It's not like me to forget a name like that. Anyway, it's the big news of the day, considering the history of the place... I wonder if he knows.
As she speaks, Sukie looks at... SUKIE'S POV - FELICIA GABRIEL comes into the office and waves "hello" as she passes Sukie. Sukie smiles and waves back.
INT. ALEX'S HOUSE - DAY
Alex on the phone, playing with one of her dolls, absentmindedly as she talks. Who's that?
Felicia?
Yes. You're very sharp today. She just walked in.
Lunch with Clyde.
INT. OFFICE - SUKIE - DAY
Yeah. I have to get to the shop. See you later. 'Bye.
She hangs up the phone and starts gathering her things.
ANGLE - FELICIA AND CLYDE
Leaving for lunch. Dressed in very conservative clothes, they look a perfect little dull couple.
That house is a landmark building. They had no right to sell it.
I can't do anything about that.
No. You're too busy reporting the last minutes of the horse trough
latest trip to Bermuda. And what's going to happen to those birds, now? Tell me that. What birds?
The snowy egrets! Where are they going to nest?
How the hell should I know? Exactly.
Exactly!
Felicia, what the hell has gotten into you? Honey, it's only a house. Why has this upset you like this?
(calms down) I have this feeling, Clyde. I have the most terrible feeling. Something... Something crawling inside me. Something evil. Only it's more than a feeling. Something is happening, Clyde.
Clyde tries to take her hand. and keeps walking. Felicia pushes him away
(continuing) Don't do that. Don't touch me like that, you silly prick. I'm not a crazy person. And I want to know what you're going to do about this...
INT. ALEX'S CAR - ALEX - DAY
is driving through town. The box full of dolls sits on the front seat next to her.
EXT. GIFT SHOP ("THE YAPPING FOX") - DAY
THROUGH the WINDOW, we can see Alex talking to MRS. BIDDLE, the owner of the shop. She is in her eighties.
INT. SHOP - MRS. BIDDLE AND ALEX
are taking the dolls from the box and putting them on a display shelf.
Well, I'm glad they're selling.
Yes. But they're not actually 'selling.' They sold. Just like that.
I don't get it.

Well, darling, you know how I adore the little creatures. So fine. So unreal and yet, the detail... little fingers and little toes and little pudenda making a bump in their dresses. So sweet. But honestly, nobody's even looked at them sideways for months. And then this morning, from absolutely nowhere, this man appeared. So charming. Not really handsome but... riveting. Yes. That's the word, I was riveted. I was looking into his eyes and I found myself thinking thoughts I hadn't entertained since World War II. I think I actually blushed. Can you imagine?
No. Well, yes. I mean... so what happened? He bought one.
He bought all of them. Every single one. How about that?
Alex is disturbed.
Well, who was he? Tourist?
No. He just bought the Lenox
House. He's moving in.
You don't by any chance remember his name, do you?
Yes. Of course. His name is... He told me. I wrote it down. It was... foreign. No. But it sounded... It's right on the tip of my tongue and I can't ... Well, isn't that diabolical? Hmnn.
INT. CHURCH MEETING HALL - JANE - NIGHT

is on the small stage of the little hall. She is playing the cello. PULL BACK to include the other three members of a string quartet. None of them play with the skill of Jane; but they are not bad. ANGLE - ALEX AND SUKIE Seated in an audience of about one hundred people on folding chairs. Like the other members of the audience, they are listening attentively. And then a sound. Like HEAVY BREATHING. Almost inaudible at first. And then SLIGHTLY LOUDER. Alex notices it first. She tries to ignore it. She concentrates on the music.
Playing hard into the climax of the piece. The sound
again. A long RUMBLE of a BREATH. Like a death rattle. Or a growl of a sleeping animal. Jane doesn't seem to hear it. Some people hear the sound. loud.

ANGLE - ALEX AND SUKIE Who jump slightly in their seats as the sound SPURTS LOUDLY for a second and then settles back to the strange, adenoidal RUMBLING. It doesn't sound human. Alex cranes her head in the direction of the menacing sound. HER POV - AUDIENCE As CAMERA PANS DOWN the rows of people. The sound gets LOUDER as we approach the source -- more sinister, more phlegmatic and sort of disgusting, too. Heads are turning as we PASS them and finally ARRIVE AT the comic sight of...
ANGLE - DARYL VAN HORNE

Asleep in his chair, his head lolling on his shoulder, his mouth open, drooling, and emitting the strangest and most sickening sound of snoring possible. Contrary to our expectations, he looks like a harmless sort of guy -- ordinary, disheveled, and, at first sight, not particularly handsome. Where the quartet finishes the piece they are playing. ANGLE - AUDIENCE Bursting into applause and coming to their feet.
ANGLE - DARYL

Startled by the applause. He wakes, tries to sit up, slips off his chair, and falls to the floor with a great clatter and bang of folding chairs. ANGLE - QUARTET Bowing to the audience. Above the applause, the sound of one person shouting, "Brava!" Jane looks in the direction of the sound.
ANGLE - DARYL
On his feet now, applauding and shouting, "Brava!" winks and smiles at Jane. Who smiles and nods at Daryl.
INT. CHURCH FOYER - NIGHT
A large area at the top of two flights of stairs. A reception is in progress after the concert -- cheese and wine in plastic cups. ANGLE - SUKIE AND ALEX
Yeah, but who is he?
ANGLE - FELICIA AND CLYDE
Well, he must have a name. He does. it.
I just can't remember
ANGLE - ALEX AND SUKIE
And why would he ask for you? Because I'm good at it. Really, Alex. You give me credit for being so stupid sometimes.
ANGLE - MRS. BIDDLE AND WOMAN Yes.
He was in my shop.
Well, who is he? Well, I don't know. But he is charming.
ANGLE - ALEX AND SUKIE
I didn't mean that you were stupid. I mean, how would he know? How would he even know who you were? I don't know. I don't know anything about him.
ANGLE - RAYMOND NEFF AND MRS. NEFF
Apparently, he bought the Lenox house. Just like that. MRS. NEFF From New York?
Yeah. But he has some kind of foreign name. 'Van.' Van something or other.
ANGLE - JANE
approaching Sukie and Alex. She is carrying a small bouquet of flowers and looking radiant. Raymond Neff stops her with congratulations and a kiss. She keeps moving until she reaches Sukie and Alex.
(showing them the bouquet) Aren't they sweet? Oh, look.
Yeah.
And the nicest note.
Sukie takes the note and reads it. Alex starts fiddling nervously with a string of pearls she's wearing.
Any name? No.
Just an initial. D. letter 'd.' Just the Yes, that's it. D. Uh, David... Daniel... Dick... No... Daryl! That's it. Daryl!
As she says the name, Alex's pearls break and fall to the floor.
Damn!
The pearls roll across the floor. to retrieve them.
ANGLE - RAYMOND AND MRS. NEFF
Van Horne! Alex starts trying
That was it.
ANGLE - MRS. BIDDLE also remembering suddenly. Yes, Daryl... MRS. BIDDLE
Rolling toward the feet of...
ANGLE - CLYDE AND FELICIA
As Clyde also remembers his name. Daryl Van Horne. That's his
What kind of name
She turns and starts to walk away from Clyde.
But before she can finish the name, she slips on the pearls and starts to fall down the stairs.
ANGLE - FELICIA (SLOW MOTION)

Falling as the word "Daryl" ECHOES like a scream through the air. And then silence, except for the pearls trickling down the stairs. CLOSE ON FELICIA lying at the bottom of the stairs. slightly and mutters... Van Horne... She raises her head
INT. ALEX'S HOUSE - ALEX - DAY

is sitting at her kitchen table having a cup of coffee. On a piece of paper, she is writing the name, Daryl Van Horne. Alex washing dishes. She stops. She dumps the dirty dishes into the sink and walks away. Alex cleaning dust from Venetian blinds. Alex making a bed. She stops before she is finished. She sits on the edge of the bed. She feels tired. She stops. Sad. And lonely. She wraps her arms around herself. She lies back on the bed and stares at the ceiling.
EXT. ROAD - DAY
In the distance, the Lenox House can be seen perched near the sea, surrounded by fields of sea grass and sand dunes.
ANGLE - ALEX'S CAR
pulls up and parks on the edge of the road. Getting out of the car. get a better look. She climbs one of the dunes to
HER POV - HOUSE
Several trucks and workmen are around. And then... No sign of Daryl.
Looking for me?
As she turns, surprised to find...
Standing beside her.
I said are you looking for me. Or is this just a happy coincidence? No.
I was just...
Just looking.
Too bad. You haven't seen any snowy egrets out here, have you?
Not that I know of.
Me, either. I'm trying to put a tennis court in and this woman, Felicia Gabriel, she sent me this letter, telling me I can't fill in the wetlands because the snowy egrets like to nest there.
That's what they say.
Well, I haven't seen any. 'Course, I wouldn't know a snowy egret if I was pissing on one. Do you want some lunch?
It's the wrong time of the year. For lunch?
For the birds.
Daryl smiles and offers his hand.
Daryl Van Horne. Hi.
You're the local sculptress. The one who makes the bubbie dolls.
They're just little things, I...
Little, yeah. But potent. Full of juice. Potent. You can feel it when you pick them up. I sent a couple down to New York. I have a friend...
He turns and starts to walk away toward the house as he talks. Alex is not sure what to do. Then she decides to follow him.

(continuing) ... down there. Runs a gallery. Nice relaxed, terrific guy. Wanted him to take a look. Of course, the scale is all wrong. You need to work bigger. Let yourself go. Get some size into it. Do you like fish? We're having fish for lunch...
EXT. TERRACE OF HOUSE - DAY

An elaborate table set with fine china and crystal. Alex and Daryl are seated at the table. Fidel is clearing the plates. Alex watches Fidel, especially the scar on his cheek. Daryl pours more wine. Fidel goes.
Interesting, huh? What?
Fidel. That's his name. Been with me forever. I don't know where the scar comes from. Women like it. They're crazy about him. Really?
He has a big schlong. Huge. Well, there you are. Scale again. Size. (MORE)
Maybe it's a masculine thing. They say women don't care. I'm sort of in the middle myself. How about you?
I beg your pardon?

You see, women are in touch with different things. That's my opinion. I know that's not a fashionable opinion right now, but fuck it, I know what I see. I see men running around, dipping their dicks into every little thing, trying to make things happen. But it's women who have the real power, isn't it? Women are the source, the only power. Nature. Birth. Re-birth. Cliche, cliche. Sure. But true.
Why are you telling me all this?

Because you're an honest woman. And I'm being honest with you. I like women. I respect them. If you want me to talk to you like you're a dumb twit, I will. But what's the point? You have brains, Alex. More than brains. You have power. And you don't even know it, do you? Well, most women don't.
Were you ever married?

Good question. You see? Brains. The answer is no. Don't believe in it. Good for the man. Lousy for the woman. She suffocates. She dies. I've seen it. And then the husband runs around complaining that he's fucking a dead person. And he's the one that killed her. Where's your husband?

Well, sorry, but you're one of the lucky ones. When a woman unloads a husband -- or when a husband unloads a woman -- however it happens -- death, desertion, divorce -- the three 'd's' -when it happens, a woman blossoms. Blooms. Like flowers. Like fruit. She is ripe. Talk about power. That's the woman for me. Would you like to see my bedroom?
who almost chokes on her wine.
INT. HOUSE - ALEX AND DARYL - DAY

moving through the cavernous rooms of the house. They are sparsely furnished. Some are empty. Some have painters working. A carpenter seems to appear from nowhere and then disappear. Daryl babbles on, giving Alex a tour of his belongings. CAMERA INCLUDES everything he mentions, but is always MOVING, producing a DIZZY EFFECT like the effect Daryl is having on Alex. Occassionally she responds with some polite noise. But she is disconnected, as if falling under a spell.

... The lab is over there, past the pianos, used to be a ballroom -- nice word, ballroom -- nothing to see right now, equipment, crates ... Over there, the study, have to unpack my books, some of the old sets have to wait, need an aircontrol unit, and an alarm system. Not that that's any insurance policy, people are crazy, wicked, drugs, all that... What's happened to the world? That's what I'd like to know...
INT. LIVING ROOM - ALEX AND DARYL

still moving. An immense, long room, doors opening to the terrace and the sea. A huge walk-in fireplace, floor to ceiling mirrors in gilt frames, etc. A few pieces of modern furniture, some Oriental rugs, stacks of paintings, leaning against the walls and several pieces of modern sculpture. ANGLE - PIECE OF SCULPTURE A naked woman lying on her back, knees bent, legs spread, concocted of chicken wire, flattened beer cans, an old porcelain chamber pot for her belly, pieces of chrome, car bumper, items of underwear stiffened with lacquer and glue.
